commits.message: feat: initial release of doc-it v0.1.0
commits.author: Sethumadhavan004
commits.summary: This commit introduces the initial release (v0.1.0) of the `doc-it` tool, which automatically generates developer session logs from git history. The core functionality involves reading git commits, summarizing changes using an LLM, detecting related past commits, and writing a structured `DEVLOG.md` file. Key files modified include `.gitignore`, `README.md`, and several `doc_it` modules like `cli.py`, `git_reader.py`, `chains.py`, `graph.py`, and `renderer.py`, alongside the `pyproject.toml` file, reflecting the foundational structure of the tool.

pointers.message: feat: M1 — git data layer and CLI skeleton
test: add readme test file to verify update mode
feat: M2 — add LangChain diff summarizer chain
feat: M3 — add renderer and wire DEVLOG.md writing
feat: M4 — add pointer detection with structured output and anchor links
feat: M5 — replace update pipeline with LangGraph StateGraph

commits.message: feat: add devlog.json manifest and fix env + dependency issues                                                                                                Body:  Introduce devlog.json as the machine-readable graph data manifest,  generated alongside DEVLOG.md on every run.  - Add get_files_changed() to git_reader for per-commit file lists  - Add write_devlog_json() to renderer with locked v1.0 schema    (sessions, commits, tags, files_changed, pointers)  - Wire JSON output into both init mode (cli.py) and update mode (graph.py)  - Move .env from phase-2/ into doc-it/ — correct project boundary  - Fix load_env() path reference accordingly  - Upgrade langchain-google-genai 2.1.12 → 4.2.1 to resolve    max_retries conflict with google-genai 1.x SDK  - Remove conflicting google-generativeai 0.8.4
commits.author: Sethumadhavan004
commits.summary: This commit introduces significant changes to the `devlog.json` manifest generation and integration into both init and update modes. The code now generates a `devlog.json` file alongside `DEVLOG.md`, detailing sessions, commits, tags, and files changed.  The `write_devlog_json` function is added to the renderer, and the JSON output is wired into `cli.py` and `graph.py`. Additionally, the project boundary was corrected by moving the `.env` file, and the langchain-google-genai dependency was upgraded to 4.2.1 to resolve a max_retries conflict.

pointers.message: feat: M3 — add renderer and wire DEVLOG.md writing
fix: made devlog.md user/llm friendly by organising its structure.

commits.message: feat: add interactive commit graph with doc-it graph and serve commands
commits.author: Sethumadhavan004
commits.summary: This commit introduces an interactive commit graph visualization tool (`doc-it graph`) that generates a `graph.html` file containing a D3 force-directed graph of the project's commit history. The tool reads `devlog.json`, backfills missing commit history if necessary, and serves the generated graph via a local HTTP server, allowing users to explore the commit graph in their browser.  The code includes commands to regenerate the graph and serve it, along with a `.gitignore` entry to exclude the generated `graph.html` file.

pointers.message: feat: initial release of doc-it v0.1.0
fix: made devlog.md user/llm friendly by organising its structure.
bugfix: Gemma model not able to process high amount of token, so stalling time between api calls to avoid rate limit.
bugfix:anchor commits not able to be identified because of unreliable json formatting by Gemma
